citations.description: Scope section in ticket body: 'NEW docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/criterion_firings.jsonl — the append-only firing ledger, backfilled from the existing corpus (committed; CI-visible)'
The file does not appear in list_directory of docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ — only criterion_effectiveness.json is present

findings.criterion: AC1 (recorder + auto-inclusion): criterion_firings.jsonl committed with >5000 rows and ≥30 distinct criterion ids
findings.detail: The Scope section explicitly lists 'NEW docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/criterion_firings.jsonl — the append-only firing ledger, backfilled from the existing corpus (committed; CI-visible).' The file is not present in the repository — it is listed in .gitignore (line 16). The proving commands for AC1 require `wc -l < runs/criterion_firings.jsonl` and `jq -r '.c[]' runs/criterion_firings.jsonl`, which cannot pass in CI or from the committed codebase. The recorder code implementation is complete and correct, but the committed artifact required by the scope and proving command is absent.
findings.remediation: Either commit a representative sample of the firing ledger (sized under 500KB) or update AC1 to not require the committed artifact, and re-verify.

citations.description: Dedup logic exists and is correct, but the proving command requires the file on disk

findings.criterion: AC2 (idempotent incremental append): re-running appends no duplicates; proof via wc -l on criterion_firings.jsonl
findings.detail: AC2's proving command requires the criterion_firings.jsonl file to exist in the repository (`N=$(wc -l < …/criterion_firings.jsonl)`). Since the file is git-ignored and absent from the committed codebase, the proving command cannot be executed in CI. The dedup logic in the code (lines 296-320 of criterion_effectiveness.py) is correct and complete, but the CI-visible artifact the AC proof depends on is missing.
findings.remediation: The dedup logic is present; the proof gap is solely the missing committed ledger file. Satisfy AC2 either by committing a ledger sample or by updating the AC to a CI-testable equivalent (e.g. a unit test of the dedup logic, which is already present in test_criterion_effectiveness.py).

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
